section.title: Overview
section.para: The rust_sst module is a clean Rust implementation of &sip; Session Timers (RFC 4028) for &osips;. It manages Session-Expires and Min-SE header negotiation, dialog lifetime management, and re-INVITE/UPDATE refresh handling.

The module supports two operating modes:
Automatic mode - when dialog.so is loaded, the module hooks into dialog lifecycle callbacks and handles all SST negotiation transparently. No script function calls are required beyond create_dialog().
Script override mode - when dialog.so is not loaded (or for per-call control), the script functions sst_check() and sst_update() allow manual Session-Expires validation and header construction.
All timer negotiation logic is extracted into pure, testable Rust functions.

section.title: Automatic mode (dialog integration)
section.para: When dialog.so is loaded, the module registers a DLGCB_CREATED callback that intercepts every new INVITE dialog. The sequence of operations:

listitem.para: On INVITE (DLGCB_CREATED): Parses Session-Expires, Min-SE, and Supported: timer headers from the request. Validates the requested interval against the configured default_min_se. If the &uac; does not include Session-Expires, the proxy inserts one using the configured default_interval. Sets the initial dialog lifetime via $DLG_timeout.

On 2XX response (DLGCB_RESPONSE_FWDED): If the &uas; includes Session-Expires, the proxy uses it (subject to the configured minimum). If the &uas; does not include Session-Expires but the &uac; supports timer, the proxy inserts Session-Expires and Require: timer headers with refresher=uac. Updates the dialog lifetime to match the negotiated interval.
On 422 response: Adjusts the internal Min-SE to the value from the 422 reply to prevent negotiation loops.
On re-INVITE/UPDATE (DLGCB_REQ_WITHIN): Parses Session-Expires from the refresh request/response and updates the dialog lifetime accordingly.
On dialog termination: Cleans up per-dialog SST state and updates statistics.

section.title: Script override mode
section.para: When dialog.so is not loaded, or when the operator wants manual control, the script functions sst_check() and sst_update() are available.

sst_check() validates the Session-Expires from an incoming INVITE against the configured minimum. If the interval is too small, it returns -1 (denied) and sets $var(sst_min_se) for use in a 422 reply.
sst_update() computes the final Session-Expires and Min-SE header values for insertion into a 200 OK response.

section.title: &osips; Modules
section.para:
The following modules must be loaded before this module:
For automatic mode:
dialog - required for dialog lifecycle callbacks and $DLG_timeout.
sipmsgops - required for append_hf() and remove_hf() used to insert/modify Session-Expires and Min-SE headers.
For script override mode:
sipmsgops - required for append_hf() in the script examples.

section.title: External Libraries or Applications
section.para:
The following libraries or applications must be installed before running &osips; with this module loaded:
None.

section.title: default_interval (integer)
section.para: Default Session-Expires interval in seconds. This is used when the &uac; does not include a Session-Expires header in the INVITE, and as the baseline for negotiation.
The effective interval is always clamped to be at least as large as default_min_se. Must be at least 90 (RFC 4028 minimum); values below 90 are clamped to 90 at module load.

section.title: default_min_se (integer)
section.para: Default Min-SE value in seconds. This represents the proxy minimum acceptable session interval. Per RFC 4028, this value must be at least 90 seconds. The module clamps any value below 90 to 90.

section.title: default_refresher (string)
section.para: Default refresher role: uac or uas. Determines which endpoint is responsible for sending session refresh requests (re-INVITEs or UPDATEs) when the &uac; does not specify a preference.

section.title: policies_file (string)
section.para: Path to a CSV file containing per-account timer policy overrides. When set, sst_check() looks up the account from $fU and uses the per-account interval, min_se, and refresher instead of the module defaults.

The file format is one entry per line: account,interval,min_se,refresher. Lines starting with # are comments. Blank lines are ignored. The interval and min_se are clamped to a minimum of 90 per RFC 4028.
The file is loaded at startup and can be reloaded at runtime using sst_reload().

section.title: adaptive_min_se (integer)
section.para: Enable adaptive Min-SE learning from 422 replies. When enabled, the module maintains a per-worker map of destination hosts to the highest Min-SE value seen in 422 replies from that host. On subsequent INVITEs to the same destination, the learned Min-SE is used instead of default_min_se if it is higher, preventing repeated 422 rejections.
Set to 1 to enable, 0 to disable.

section.title: force_refresher (string)
section.para: Force the refresher role regardless of negotiation. When set to uac or uas, the module always uses this refresher in Session-Expires headers, overriding whatever the endpoints negotiate. This is useful for endpoints that advertise timer support but fail to send session refresh requests.
Set to an empty string (or omit) to disable and allow normal negotiation.

example.title: Set force_refresher parameter
example.programlisting: ... # Force the proxy (UAS side) to always be the refresher modparam("rust_sst", "force_refresher", "uas") ...

section.title: publish_events (integer)
section.para: Enable event publishing. When set to 1, the module publishes significant events as NOTICE-level structured log messages that operators can consume via syslog or log aggregation. If the NATS module is loaded, events are also published to NATS subjects under opensips.events.*.
Events published by this module: E_SST_EXPIRED (on session timeout) and E_SST_STALE (when a session should have been refreshed but was not). Each event includes a JSON payload with relevant details.

Validate the Session-Expires and Min-SE values from an incoming INVITE. The function parses the Session-Expires and Min-SE headers from the &sip; message and checks them against the provided or configured minimums.
On return, sets $var(sst_interval), $var(sst_min_se), and $var(sst_refresher) for use in script logic.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: interval (string) - Override for the requested interval. Pass 0 to use the value from the Session-Expires header.
min_se (string) - Override for the minimum session interval. Pass 0 to use the configured default_min_se.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: 1 - interval is acceptable (at or above the effective minimum).
-1 (denied) - interval is too small. $var(sst_min_se) contains the value to use in a 422 reply.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE.

example.title: sst_check usage

section.para: Compute the Session-Expires and Min-SE header values for a 200 OK response. The function applies defaults and enforces minimum values.
On return, sets $var(sst_session_expires) to the full Session-Expires header value (e.g., 1800;refresher=uas) and $var(sst_min_se) to the Min-SE value.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: interval (string) - The desired Session-Expires interval. Pass 0 to use the configured default_interval.
min_se (string) - The desired Min-SE value. Pass 0 to use the configured default_min_se.
refresher (string) - The refresher role: uac or uas. Defaults to default_refresher if unrecognized.

section.para: Return codes:
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: sst_update usage

section.para: Retrieve a JSON array listing all active SST-tracked dialogs on the current worker process. Sets $var(sst_status) with a JSON array where each element contains: call_id, interval, refresher, time_remaining, and refresh_count.
Return codes:
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.
Retrieve module statistics as a JSON string. Sets $var(sst_stats) with a JSON object containing all tracked counters for the current worker process.
Return codes:
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.
Reload the per-account timer policies from the file specified by policies_file. The reload is atomic: readers see either the old or the new data, never a partial state.
On success, sets $var(sst_reload_count) to the number of policy entries loaded.
Return codes:

listitem.para: -1 -- reload failed (file read error or no policies_file configured).

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: sst_reload usage

section.para: Retrieve module statistics in Prometheus text exposition format. Sets $var(sst_prom) with a multi-line string containing all counters formatted with # HELP, # TYPE, and metric value lines suitable for scraping by a Prometheus server.
Return codes:
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: sst_prometheus usage
example.programlisting: ... sst_prometheus(); # $var(sst_prom) now contains Prometheus text format ...

section.title: Exported Pseudo-Variables
section.para: The following pseudo-variables are set by the script functions:

section.title: $var(sst_interval)
section.para: The negotiated Session-Expires interval in seconds. Set by sst_check().

section.title: $var(sst_min_se)
section.para: The effective Min-SE value in seconds. Set by both sst_check() and sst_update(). For a 422 reply, use this value in the Min-SE header.

section.title: $var(sst_refresher)
section.para: The negotiated refresher role (uac or uas). Set by sst_check().

section.title: $var(sst_session_expires)
section.para: The complete Session-Expires header value including the refresher parameter (e.g., 1800;refresher=uas). Set by sst_update(). Use this directly in append_hf().

section.title: $var(sst_status)
section.para: JSON array string listing all active SST-tracked dialogs on the current worker. Each element contains call_id, interval, refresher, time_remaining, and refresh_count. Set by sst_status().

section.title: $var(sst_stats)
section.para: JSON string containing the module statistics for the current worker process. Set by sst_stats().

section.title: $var(sst_prom)
section.para: Prometheus text exposition format string containing all module statistics. Set by sst_prometheus().

section.title: $var(sst_reload_count)
section.para: The number of per-account policy entries loaded after a successful sst_reload() call.
Number of currently active SST-managed dialog sessions (automatic mode only).
Number of dialog sessions that expired via the session timer (automatic mode only).
Number of 422 Session Interval Too Small replies observed (automatic mode) or conditions detected (script mode).

Number of sessions detected as stale at termination time. A session is considered stale when the time since the last refresh (re-INVITE or UPDATE) exceeds the negotiated Session-Expires interval, indicating the refreshing endpoint failed to send timely refreshes.
Number of Session-Expires or Min-SE headers inserted or modified by the module (automatic mode only).
Number of sessions where the UAC was negotiated as the refresher (responsible for sending re-INVITEs or UPDATEs). Incremented at dialog creation based on the initial negotiation result.
Number of sessions where the UAS was negotiated as the refresher. Incremented at dialog creation based on the initial negotiation result.

Return the table of currently tracked SST dialogs. Entries are populated by the module's DLGCB_CREATED callback (from a worker) and removed by the DLGCB_TERMINATED / DLGCB_EXPIRED / DLGCB_FAILED callbacks.

The backing table lives in OpenSIPS shared memory (allocated pre-fork), so every worker process and the MI process (rank -2) observe the same state. Calling sst_show via opensips-cli or the MI HTTP/JSON-RPC endpoint returns the same aggregate view regardless of which process handled the request.

The response is a JSON object:
dialogs — array of per-dialog records with the following fields:
callid — SIP Call-ID (up to 128 bytes).
se_interval — negotiated Session-Expires interval in seconds (falls back to default_interval when the underlying dialog lifetime is unavailable).
min_se — effective Min-SE value in seconds (minimum of 90 per RFC 4028).
refresher — uac, uas, or none.
created_unix — dialog start time (UNIX seconds).
expires_unix — expected expiry (created_unix + se_interval).
remaining — max(0, expires_unix - now) in seconds.
expired — true when remaining reaches zero.
count — length of the dialogs array.
The command takes no parameters.
